lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY guarded, light (NOT yet runtime-proven). Closes both devopsservice writes: POST /v1/devops-services (createdevopsservice) and DELETE /v1/devops-services/{devops_service_id} (deletedevopsservice). A devops-service is a control-plane CI/CD workspace (no obviously-billable infra), so this attempts a guarded REAL create -> capture id -> delete; but because the body needs a live IAM member id (members[]>=1, credential precondition) it is coverage-only: the create is optional+broad expect_status so a 400/422 (missing/wrong field) still records coverage without failing the run. devops_service_id is captured from a 2xx create when possible, else soft-captured from the read-only list so the DELETE and id-bound GETs still target a real id. The check-duplication GET pre-checks the chosen tenant_code/tenant_name. probe-reads exercises showdevopsservice + checkdeletabledevopsservice. 2026-06-14 (IB-019 / PF-05): reconciled with the corrected resource model (knowledge/formal/resources/devops-tools__devopsservice.yaml + data/api_docs.json DevOpsServiceCreateRequest). The previous create body {name, description} was the WRONG SHAPE (caused the live 400 ValidationError ['Field required']x3, run 27394211896) and is replaced by the real required members/tenant_code/tenant_name. check-duplication param was the wrong guess ?name= -> corrected to ?tenant_code=&tenant_name= (both optional query, per api_docs). List soft-capture $.contents[0].id was WRONG -> corrected to $.devops_services[0].id (DevOpsServiceListResponse.devops_services[].id, response_example confirmed). Create capture $.devops_service.id is CONFIRMED against DevOpsServiceShowResponse{devops_service: DevOpsService}.id.

steps.name: check-name-duplication
steps._note: checkduplicationdevopsservice: query params tenant_code/tenant_name (both optional, pattern ^[a-z0-9\-]*$), result:boolean. Corrected from the previous WRONG ?name= param (no `name` query exists on this endpoint).

steps.name: list-devops-services
steps._note: Soft-capture an existing id so the DELETE / id-bound GETs target a real resource even if the guarded create below 4xx-es. Corrected from the previous WRONG capture $.contents[0].id — the response envelope is DevOpsServiceListResponse.devops_services[] (DevOpsService.id), confirmed by api_docs response_example.

steps.name: create-devops-service
steps._note: Guarded create. Body = DevOpsServiceCreateRequest real required fields members(array[string],>=1)/tenant_code/tenant_name (tags optional, omitted) — replaces the previous INVENTED {name, description} (the PF-05 400 'Field required'x3 cause). tenant_code/tenant_name share pattern ^[a-z0-9\-]*$ ({ualpha} is lowercase-guaranteed). members[] is a credential precondition: it needs >=1 live IAM user id that this run cannot supply, so it is sent EMPTY — which deterministically yields the expected 400 ('members' Field required) and RECORDS coverage WITHOUT creating a real workspace (the list-captured id covers DELETE). _note: to actually create a devops-service in a credentialed live run, seed members[] with a real IAM user id. Capture $.devops_service.id is CONFIRMED against the create response model DevOpsServiceShowResponse{devops_service: DevOpsService}.id (api_docs response_example).

steps.name: delete-devops-service
steps._note: destructive — only runs under SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE. Deletes the captured/soft-captured devops-service. optional+broad so a 4xx records coverage; on a shared account this may target a soft-captured pre-existing id, so it is never required to succeed (expected 403/404).
